scope: Random, group, temporal — when each applies; ties to the cutoff from ch01_s05.
key_concepts: A proper data split acts as the quarantine mechanism preventing leakage before feature extraction.
Random split works only for observations that are completely independent of one another.
Group split is required when the dataset contains clustered records, such as multiple visit records for the same patient. These must be separated by entity group, not randomly by row.
Temporal split is necessary for time-related data. A strict cutoff point prevents random selection from assigning future data into the training set.

definitions.definition: Pemisahan baris observasi secara acak tanpa mempertimbangkan klaster atau waktu.
Pemisahan observasi berdasarkan himpunan atau anggota dari subjek unik yang sama.
Pemisahan data berdasarkan batas pengurutan periode waktu kronologis dari kejadian berjalan.

examples: A medical dataset has 10 X-rays each for 100 patients. A simple random split might put X-rays of patient A in both train and test. A group split forces all patient A X-rays strictly into one set.

relations.builds_upon: ch01_s05 (unit definitions lead to group/temporal needs)
relations.builds_toward: ch02_s06 (how to use these inside CV)
